name: MIT-AI-Fall20
hook: archived problem sets from mit 6.034 intro ai (fall 2020): search, csp, games, k-nn/decision trees, neural nets, svms, bayes nets, boosting, each lab with its own tester. a personal time-capsule of coursework, kept as-is.

name: MIT-SDM-Thesis-on-System-Dynamics-Modeling-of-Bitcoin
hook: the model files and data pipeline for vignesh's mit sdm thesis modeling bitcoin as a system-dynamics stock-and-flow system (vensim dss 9.2.4 .mdl plus calibration .voc/.out/.rep files and a python data-pull script). an academic artifact preserved at submission state.
demo_cmd: open thesis_checkpoint.mdl in Vensim DSS 9.2.4 (load DataFiles/ConsolidatedData.vdfx)

name: earnings-pillar-diff
hook: event-driven cli that diffs hyperscaler 10-Q capex and purchase-obligation line items quarter-over-quarter, maps deltas onto named investment-thesis pillars, and renders a one-page action memo. for equity analysts tracking ai-infra capex theses.

name: leetcode
hook: a collection of 21 leetcode solutions filed by problem number in python and sql, no write-ups. self-declared archived grind, kept for completeness.

name: News-Bias-Multi-Agent-Pipeline
hook: a framing-brief generator that pulls an article set on a subject, summarizes, detects framing/bias, critiques the detection, and reconciles into one reader-facing brief with verbatim-verified citations. the same pipeline is implemented three ways (static python, langchain, langgraph) as an agent-orchestration teaching artifact; explicitly not a production bias detector.

name: agent-notary-layer
hook: a schema-first repo defining a tamper-evident receipt format for cross-org agent actions (PO commit, payment auth, redline accept), shipped as a JSON Schema plus a reference verifier CLI and a conformance suite. for teams needing an auditable agent-to-agent action trail (regulated finance/procurement).

name: ai-supply-chain-copilot-prd
hook: a markdown-first product teardown and prd for an ai supply-chain exception-triage copilot (nine numbered docs: problem through build plan), paired with a small static vite/react prototype showing an exception queue with cited evidence and approval-gated actions on synthetic data. for hiring managers and eng leaders assessing tpm judgment.

name: commit-provenance
hook: intended to produce cryptographically signed, machine-verifiable per-commit author attribution for AI-mixed codebases (human vs AI-authored vs AI-reviewed). today it ships only a CLI that reports which readiness artifacts exist in the repo. for regulated eng teams and maintainers facing AI-PR floods.

name: dream-replay-cli
hook: an offline-cognition CLI that ingests a week of agent traces plus a spec ledger and decision ledger and emits a human-reviewable dreams/YYYY-WNN/ folder of candidate skills, memory updates, tests, and spec amendments â€” proposals only, promotion is human-gated. for builders running multi-agent portfolios who have traces but no review cadence.

name: review-queue
hook: cli plus typed promotion-record schema for human-gating ai-generated candidates (memory updates, tests, briefs, contracts): a candidate lands in a queue, a reviewer approves/rejects/edits with diff and provenance, approved items ship to a target. for editorial ops and prompt-engineering teams running production ai content.

name: capital-build-reconciler
hook: a monthly pipeline that ingests personal LLM-inference telemetry (rate-limit hits, dollar-per-token drift, region latency) and reconciles it against six AI-infra capital pillars to emit HOLD/UPWEIGHT/TRIM verdicts. for one portfolio operator tracking AI-infra exposure against private build telemetry.
demo_cmd: python -m capital_reconcile ingest --provider anthropic --raw tests/fixtures --out reports/2026-M06.fixture.parquet

name: channel-atlas
hook: a fixture-backed catalog of hyperscaler off-balance-sheet AI-infra financing (JV/SPV/private-credit deals) built into a counterparty graph tied to specific data-center campuses, emitted as a markdown report plus graph JSON. for credit/equity analysts and CFOs assessing AI-infra supplier-concentration risk.

name: grid-silicon
hook: phantom-vs-real capacity engine for announced US data-center gigawatts â€” parses an ercot large-load queue fixture and emits a 0-100 realness score per project with five evidence items, shipped as a jsonl report. for power/semis equity desks and infra credit underwriters.

name: sovereign-compute
hook: cross-country feasibility scorecard for sovereign-ai compute programs (uae g42, saudi humain, eu ai factories, indiaai, uk aisi): scores each announced national build-out on silicon/power/water/talent, reports projected real GW vs announced GW and the binding constraint per program. for sovereign-program managers, defense planners, and sovereign-wealth-fund infra teams.

name: supplier-risk-rag-agent
hook: a citation-faithful rag agent over sec edgar filings: a deterministic hybrid ranker plus a post-hoc verbatim citation verifier, exposed as a byok streamlit app, gated by four named eval suites that run with no vendor keys. for procurement/supplier-risk readers and rag practitioners studying citation faithfulness as a separate gate.
demo_cmd: python -m uv run streamlit run app.py

name: puc-docket-rag
hook: a citation-faithful retrieval index over public state-puc and ferc dockets: ingest, chunk, stdlib tf-idf index, search, and structured cost-allocation-rule extraction with citation validation. the evidence layer feeding ratepayer-exposure and site-atlas.

name: site-atlas
hook: static astro site that renders the grid-silicon dataset for the public: one page per announced us data center with its energization-confidence score, the five evidence items behind it, projected energization year, and source citations. for local journalists, county supervisors, puc commissioners, and ratepayer advocates.

name: oulipo-memory-deck
hook: a static narrative card deck where eight everyday-object base lines are run through a deterministic oulipo s+7 noun swap to seed a 40-80 word vignette, rendered to a single no-javascript html page. a personal creative-writing constraint tool.

name: promotion-vs-pip
hook: a 36-card brechtian corporate-satire print-and-play deckbuilder: cards live in yaml, a node script validates them against a json schema and renders a printable html sheet. the artifact is a paper deck plus a rules sheet.
demo_cmd: npm run validate && npm run render

name: starforge-choicescript-demo
hook: full released act 1 route of starforge canticles adapted into a stat-forward choicescript browser game, with a source-to-scene generator and quicktest/randomtest harness. runnable via a local static server.

name: starforge-narrative-tools
hook: python toolkit behind the starforge canticles serial: prose-to-game conversion schemas plus deterministic validation gates that keep the act 1 game adaptation in sync with published prose. for the author and anyone reviewing the cluster's tooling.

name: starforge-renpy-demo
hook: act 1 of the starforge canticles serial rendered as a ren'py visual-novel demo with branching scenes, dialogue, combat vignettes and relationship state. python release gate validates source without the engine; ren'py sdk needed to actually play it.

name: starforge-rpg-prototype
hook: act 1 of starforge canticles prototyped as a data-driven godot 4 rpg with party management, combat, branching dialogue, quests and save/load. python checks (validate_project, playtest_audit, validate_balance) pass without godot; godot needed to run the game.

name: starforge-twine-demo
hook: full act 1 route of starforge canticles as a single-html twine/sugarcube interactive-fiction demo, built with tweego and smoke-tested with playwright. a prebuilt build/index.html is checked in and playable in a browser.

name: interconnect-alpha
hook: queue-survival and capacity-curve analytics module for grid interconnection queues â€” kaplan-meier probability-of-COD forecasts with a brier backtest and a 2027-2030 capacity-price fan chart, shipped as the analytics layer inside gridsilicon. for power hedge funds, ipp developers, and infra debt funds.

name: policy-replay
hook: replays a proposed power-market rule change (FERC/ISO/PUC, e.g. PJM capacity-market reform) against a historical queue/auction fixture and forecasts the outcome distribution before the rule goes effective. for grid planning staff, PUC commissioners, and IPP developers.

name: ratepayer-exposure
hook: a small public astro calculator estimating how much of a pjm-zone resident's 2026-2030 electricity bill increase is attributable to data-center load growth, with every assumption cited. the discipline is no number renders without a source.

name: LLM-evaluation-framework
hook: an llm eval framework that scores a model output through three independent lenses (deterministic rules, persona raters, llm-as-judge) instead of one number, with yaml-configured suites, html/json reports, regression diffing, and ci exit-code gating. for engineers building product-grade eval pipelines.
demo_cmd: pip install -e ".[dev]"; llm-evals run eval_suites/customer_support/ --stage all --output html

name: ai-field-brief
hook: a weekly ai digest published as markdown briefs (one folder per iso week) rendered by a next.js static site with rss/atom/json feeds and an optional resend email digest. for builder-tpms who want concrete weekly moves with worked artifacts rather than news summaries.

name: brief-calibration
hook: a calibration harness that Brier-scores a weekly Field Brief's explicit predictions against later outcomes and renders a quarterly calibration memo plus a 'framings to retire' list. for the single author of a prediction-shaped publication who wants scoring discipline.

name: brief-matrix
hook: a multi-tenant control plane that scores weekly briefs from other repos on three axes (voice discipline, citation coverage, section conformance) and appends a calibration row per run; a scorer, not a generator. for editors/platform leads running spec-driven brief workflows.

name: eval-forge
hook: intended github-app-plus-cli that generates a recall@5 / citation-faithfulness / abstention / refusal / tool-call eval pack for a customer rag or agent repo and gates PRs on regressions. for ai teams who want evals that block merges, not dashboards.

name: mcp-security-lab
hook: static risk scanner for mcp server configs: flags command execution, broad filesystem scope, unauthenticated remote transports, and prompt-injection language, then writes json/markdown policy reports. for security reviewers and agent-platform engineers.

name: modelswap-replay
hook: local release gate for llm model swaps â€” samples recent route traffic, replays it offline against a candidate release, scores the replay, and writes a decision record with a pre-declared revert threshold. for ml platform teams gating model rollouts.

name: proof-gate-runner
hook: a drop-in github action plus stdlib-only python entry point that runs deterministic typed proof gates (voice_lint, spec_check) on a repo's pull requests. for oss maintainers and portfolio builders handling ai-generated pr volume.

name: source-decay-ledger
hook: typed registry over a high-volume reading list (~170 brief sources) that scores each source weekly by signal-yield against the author's published brief items, keeps an append-only item-to-source provenance ledger, and emits a KEEP/DROP/PROBATION memo. a personal forcing function for the author plus a reusable discipline for anyone running a large source list.

name: trace-ledger-spec
hook: json-schema, version-pinned controlled vocabulary, and a pure-python reference validator for an append-only ledger of agent trace events (content-addressable event_id, monotonic seq, prev_hash chaining). ships four worked example ledgers (one clean, three with planted bugs) the validator correctly catches. for teams building auditable agent-trace storage.

name: trace-to-eval-cli
hook: a local cli that ingests an agent trace (langgraph jsonl fixture), runs deterministic eval checks, and emits json/markdown reports plus a regression compare. for builders who want a repeatable pass/fail trace eval with no network or keys.

name: trace-to-eval-harness
hook: python cli that turns a failed ai trace into a checked-in deterministic eval case and regression report, validates cdcp run-evidence packets, and wraps runs in runtime-agnostic bundles for replay/adapter comparison. for eval curators and review agents. no model key needed.
demo_cmd: python -m trace_to_eval run examples/eval_cases.yaml --traces examples/traces --out reports/run.json

name: sports-prediction-os
hook: v0 scaffold for a soccer event-data analysis os built around possession value (vaep/xt via socceraction) with an understat fetcher and a planned streamlit surface. for an analytical, non-betting demo.

name: Robust-Facility-Location
hook: an interactive streamlit explorer for facility location under demand uncertainty: solves nominal, polyhedral-robust, ellipsoidal, and adaptive formulations (pulp/cbc + cvxpy) and stress-tests each with monte carlo across thousands of scenarios. for learning robust optimization tradeoffs.
demo_cmd: pip install -r requirements.txt; streamlit run app.py

name: dispatch-optimizer
hook: an event-driven simulator for constrained logistics dispatch that runs greedy sequential vs hungarian batch assignment on the same world (driver shifts, temperature/equipment constraints, pickup-and-delivery routes, deadlines) and shows where greedy falls behind. fastapi backend with a browser ui. for people studying assignment/routing tradeoffs.
demo_cmd: pip install -r requirements.txt; python -m uvicorn app.main:app --reload

name: robust-siting-lab
hook: small reproducible benchmark for joint grid/water/silicon data-center siting risk: given candidate sites and risk weights it picks the lowest-expected-cost site and reports the regret of the announced choice. for siting-risk analysts and as an optimization fixture for sibling compute repos.

name: world-food-program-robust-simulator
hook: a streamlit walkthrough of humanitarian food logistics under uncertainty on a real wfp syria scenario (mit 15.094): nominal lp, budget-constrained triage, robust socp, monte carlo validation, and two-stage adaptive contracts via cvxpy. for teaching the cost-of-robustness tradeoff.
demo_cmd: pip install -r requirements.txt; streamlit run app.py

name: AthenaTheOwl
hook: the github profile README repo â€” the portfolio's front-door index linking active/solved/drawer repos, plus a check_no_bom.py guard. not an application.

name: athena-site
hook: the personal portfolio hub: an astro site with a portfolio door grid (src/data/doors.json), long-form mdx essays, and embeds of chip-supply-chain-map and procurement-negotiation-lab. also hosts the cross-repo cdcp control-plane charter. for visitors and hiring managers entering the portfolio.

name: pattern-index
hook: mines DEC decision-ledger frontmatter across the portfolio for cross-domain pattern applications (procurement-to-AI and back), tags each with a 90-day-later outcome, and writes a quarterly retro. for the user to track which patterns actually transfer between repos.

name: portfolio-manifest
hook: a manifest format plus audit runner that walks ten-plus repos, checks each against declared artifact contracts (decision/spec/dream schema versions, voice-lint banlist, eval coverage, decision freshness), and emits a weekly markdown health snapshot. for multi-repo maintainers and platform teams.

name: portfolio-thesis-plane
hook: generates a weekly machine-computed report card per portfolio repo (thesis-alive score from five typed factors, last-run-evidence date, eval-coverage delta, decision staleness) and forces an ATTEND/FREEZE/RETIRE verdict, plus a top-2/bottom-3 rollup. for the user's weekly portfolio planning.

name: pre-mortem-ledger
hook: runs a monthly forced pre-mortem on the largest active investing position: names 3-5 failure modes, attaches an observability trigger to each, and tracks their status month-over-month against subsequent evidence. for the user's concentrated investing book.
demo_cmd: python -m uv run premortem ledger list

name: release-pillar-mapper
hook: maps one significant frontier-model release into a typed report against three downstream systems the user runs â€” the repo portfolio, the published brief corpus, and investing thesis pillars â€” emitting a per-target action queue. for vignesh's release-triage.

name: repo-position-coupling-index
hook: a monthly markdown index linking repo-portfolio slugs to investing thesis-pillar ids: validates report front matter, renders it, computes five flag types, and diffs month-to-month. for the user reconciling build bets against investing bets.
demo_cmd: python -m uv run coupling validate coupling_index/2026-M07.md

name: repo-triage
hook: a monthly forcing-function memo across the ~20 active portfolio repos: a hand-scorable 5-factor rubric forces exactly 2 ATTEND, 3 RETIRE, rest FREEZE, with a click cli to emit scoring stubs, render the memo, and run four merge gates. for the portfolio operator.

name: thesis-pillar-tracker
hook: monthly data-report tool for a single investing thesis: decomposes the thesis into 6-12 named falsifiable pillars, logs accepted evidence (CONFIRMS/REFUTES) against each, and writes one monthly review file plus a quarterly binding-constraint snapshot. for an individual investor running a disciplined thesis review.

name: multitier-psi
hook: reference private-set-intersection protocol over public supplier-graph fixtures â€” maps party-local supplier names to canonical ids, traverses tier-2/3 dependencies, and runs a two-party intersection in a plaintext baseline and a dh-based reference mode. a local testbed for procurement teams exploring shared single-source exposure without revealing full supplier lists.

name: negotiation-mechanism-replay
hook: turns one sanitized negotiation event into a five-section mechanism-design replay report (incentive map, leakage list, implied equilibrium, cross-application to portfolio repos), with a redaction gate that refuses raw/private paths. for the user's own post-hoc analysis of negotiations.

name: power-ppa-forge
hook: an open mechanism spec, reference simulator, and white paper for multi-hyperscaler power-purchase-agreement syndication on long-duration generation, using a multi-buyer Vickrey-like auction with bounded leakage so co-offtakers never reveal load profiles. for energy project-finance and mechanism-design audiences.

name: procurement-negotiation-lab
hook: a for-fun procurement-coordination simulator and mechanism-design workbench: a react/vite web app (play/lab/study) backed by a deterministic python engine that compares jit baseline, centralized oracle, admm, weighted-nash, and mpc mechanisms. for people learning why local procurement decisions leave joint surplus on the table.

name: procurement-pattern-library
hook: a typed markdown library of procurement / supply-chain / mechanism-design patterns, each paired with application cases from ai-build work, plus a python cli that validates the typing, scores transfer, and renders quarterly retros. a personal knowledge primitive for vignesh.

name: sealed-bid-sourcing
hook: reference benchmark for sealed-bid reverse procurement: compares a sealed receipt-producing runtime (opens only winning bids, keeps losing bids out of buyer output) against an unsealed markup baseline on the same 10-supplier/5-lot scenario, and reports the surplus delta. for mechanism-design researchers and procurement-tooling teams.

name: binding-constraint
hook: a deterministic diagnostic that takes a published capex plan and ranks which physical input (chip allocation, transformer lead time, water rights, labor, permits) is most likely to bind it; v0.1 ships one checked-in TSMC Arizona case and emits a sourced JSONL report. for an analyst/operator tracking semis buildout risk.

name: chip-supply-chain-map
hook: an interactive cytoscape dependency graph of the global semiconductor supply chain (78 nodes, 180 edges) with a chokepoint heuristic, nine composable shock scenarios, sourced financial-sensitivity records, and a watchlist risk-packet export. for industry-curious readers, students, and analysts exploring chokepoints.

name: fab-risk-radar
hook: scores 90-day disruption probability for tier-2/tier-3 semiconductor nodes (abf substrate, advanced-packaging osats) with three evidence items per score, rendering a monthly report. for semis procurement, resilience leads, and defense primes.

name: facility-war
hook: deterministic supply-shock simulator over public supplier graphs â€” loads a bom graph and a scenario, walks tier-1..4 dependencies, applies substitution edges, and writes a monte-carlo run record plus markdown report. for supply-chain resilience analysts stress-testing semis bills of materials.

name: semiconductor-e2e-manufacturing-optimization
hook: an interactive streamlit app for fabless wafer sourcing under uncertainty: nominal, static-robust (bertsimas-sim), two-stage adaptive (vertex enumeration), and approximate ellipsoidal models over a 5d uncertainty set, with monte carlo on yield/cost/revenue. for studying robust supply-chain decisions in semis.
demo_cmd: pip install -r requirements.txt; python -m streamlit run app.py

name: wafer-to-watt
hook: parses a checked-in edgar 8-k html fixture into a typed accelerator-commitment graph (cowos/hbm allocation edges with citations), writes a jsonl snapshot and a quarterly markdown report, and validates it. for semis analysts wanting a citation-anchored allocation snapshot.
